title: iiPythonx/iiPythonx
about: The repository I use for editing my GitHub profile, although it also keeps all of my Linux dotfiles.

title: Website (2020)
about: The original implementation of my website, hosted at iipython.cf.

title: Prism
about: The only discord bot you will ever need (satire), died after nearly 3 years of hard work on it. I quit making discord bots afterwards due to Discord's addition of the intent system.

title: RoBot
about: Keypress-based scriptable automation system for the Roblox client. All it was able to do was move around a little and was extremely janky, but yet it's my most starred repo.

title: Sprint
about: A custom shell meant to be minimal with commands, color support, multi-line editing, and more.

title: Throwaway Keys
about: A project that I designed to create secret keys for use in projects without needing to manually generate one.

title: Advent of Code
about: Project to handle my source code solutions for the 2021 Advent of Code.

title: Autofix
about: Background script to execute AUTORUN.INF files on flash drives, similar to old versions of Windows.

title: Cubic
about: A discord bot with an extensive economy system, designed partially as a test of new features before being implemented into Prism.

title: Custom Presence
about: Program that allowed users to set a custom discord status using pypresence.

title: DOS-Style Website
about: A website that was very reminiscent of the DOS era of computing. Page layout and system was incredibly inspired by qtqt's website from 2021 which you can see here.

title: Fetch
about: CLI tool to download files, similar to wget and curl, but written in pure Python with no dependencies. Never got killed, but I stopped working on it after dmed me and I ended up doing something else.

title: File Archive
about: A website that allowed me to archive files in a root folder and then authorize specific accounts to access it. The theme was based on NES.css, and ran for about 5 months.

title: Honknet
about: Honknet was a collaboration between me and DmmD where we attempted to make a chat system. It was later converted to Plasma, which died a year later.

title: JAUS
about: Just Another Url Shortener, a flask website that literally just acted as an old ass url shortener. Killed because it was slow and useless.

title: Minicraft RP
about: Program that provided discord rich presence for people playing Minicraft+.

title: Modbot
about: A discord bot that was centered around moderation, mostly chat filtering and was used for a very short time in a private discord server.

title: MyShell
about: A customizable shell program that allowed you to load custom 'kernels', giving full control over what was ran.

title: Neutron
about: A web logging system, complete with web interface and Python module. It would hook into your code and send all tracebacks to the server of your choosing for remote viewing.

title: OPP
about: A package manager that was intended to be cross platform and similar to apt.

title: PyHTTPFS
about: Flask based system that was meant to act as a static file sharing tool. Killed and removed because people kept using it and it was insecure.

title: PyNet (v1)
about: A raw socket service that allowed sharing 'webpages' using a TUI client along with a web interface for controlling and administering the pages.

title: PyNet (v2)
about: Basically a perfect copy of PyNet v1, but with a version bump and client changes.

title: PyNet (v3)
about: A full rewrite of PyNet, including a client, server, custom byte protocol, tooling, and web services to manage it all. This variant was never completed, but the remnants of it still linger in my head from time to time.

title: Sprint v2
about: Attempted rewrite of Sprint, but I never found the time to fix it. It was also plagued by unicode escape color bugs.

title: Tempmon
about: Script I made to monitor my CPU temps, because my PC was overheating while trying to play Half-Life 2.

title: Time Lock
about: Terminal app that utilized tapping keys in a timed pattern in order to unlock or access data.

title: Webchat
about: Web chat system I made while in school suspension so I could talk to my friends easier then trying to get on discord. The database was implemented with Redis, which was a mistake but it was my first time using it.

title: Windows Cleaner
about: Script that used powershell and registry editing to clean up and debloat a stock Windows 10 system.

title: iiPython.py
about: Python library where I shoved a bunch of 'useful' primitives, including a socket library, terminal utilities/coloring, and a keypress system.

title: Prism v3
about: The final variation of Prism, featuring slash commands, intents, and all the other garbage that Discord implemented. Killed because I grew sick and tired of making Discord bots.

title: iiPython Auth
about: A custom implementation of an OAuth2 providing system. Killed before being implemented into any other projects.

title: Bongo Shell
about: Attempt at a bash-compatible shell written in Nim, failed due to lack of motivation.

title: Dioxide
about: Tool that locks students out of ActiveStudent accounts. Had a week of weaponization against my local school district, before being shut down to prevent expulsion.

title: Disk Speedtest
about: Very small CLI tool that benchmarks connected flash drives using hdparm.

title: Down Site
about: A 'system offline' website that was meant to compliment my 2022 flat site design.

title: eDOS
about: Emulated operating system that was intended to be stored completely in a tarball.

title: English Sucks
about: Project that would run the Minecraft Java 1.18 language file through Google Translate around 7 times, then saved the result to a resource pack to make funny item names.

title: Enigma
about: TUI based Engima machine that was implemented using pyenigma as a school project.

title: Fedora95
about: Collection of bash scripts that turned Fedora Rawhide into a Chicago95 primed environment.

title: Hamsters
about: A project that was intended to essentially be an in-terminal game engine powered by pixel graphics. You were supposed to be able to scan a QR code visible on your screen and load a game off of that 'cartridge'. Killed because I couldn't find a good way to read from the screen.

title: Haze Cipher
about: A cipher that was intended to be reversible and hard to crack. It ended up being neither of those things, but at least I tried; it was removed from GitHub to prevent people from using it for serious data.

title: iiPythonator
about: Discord self-bot that was meant to play music and music only, and it did a damn good job of that.

title: iiPython Intranet
about: A web interface that would spin up reverse proxies at runtime for projects and handle authentication. I killed it off due to lack of time and motivation.

title: Website (2022-23)
about: The source code for my 2022-2023 website, which allowed users to define 'templates' and could switch their website template at any time. Some of my examples include my bear website and my flat design.

title: Image Loader v2
about: A Python web server that allowed Roblox servers to request images as comma seperated RGB values, so the image could be recreated using parts in-game.

title: Launch+
about: A single page app designed with Nitrogen (also dead) to act as Kiosk and provide apps such as weather access.

title: Nitrogen
about: Python module that was similar to Electron, allowing you to open web pages in Qt and send data to the Python backend.

title: p2p
about: Project that allowed sending files over an HTTP tunnel between two devices using Peer to Peer networking.

title: PHAT
about: A hardware based key system that was meant to use USB flash drives as authentication devices.

title: Rickroll
about: A flask site that literally just rickrolls you, nothing else.

title: Roblox Status Live
about: Collection of web services for monitoring the status of Roblox's vast network of servers and APIs. Included a collection agent, storage system, and frontend.

title: Service Launcher
about: CLI tool that acted very similar to systemd units. It took a config file defining what to run, and provided a daemon/CLI to interact with the processes.

title: Start Page
about: A startpage I made that was designed to be quick and minimal, originally designed for a member of one of my group chats.

title: StreamX
about: A project I helped design that acted as a self-hosted alternative to Roblox's StreamingEnabled. The original devforum thread is still available here.

title: Virtual Assistant
about: Python program that acted as a personal assistant running locally on your PC. No AI was used in it, I was using wildcard pattern matching which was just as bad as you can imagine.

title: WebTransfer
about: File transferring web application built with Flask, you just had to make an account and add a friend to send any sized file to them.

title: x2 Website
about: The official website for x2, which allowed users to publish packages and download others. At this point its useless, and x2 has turned into xpp.

title: xpp
about: Complete rewrite and modernization of both the X Programming Language, and what was formerly known as x2. Custom programming language inspired by Batch, x86 Assembly, and others.

title: 2023 Countdown
about: A project designed to perform a countdown until New Years 2023. Reused in 2024, with varying degrees of success due to date processing issues.

title: Album Downloader
about: Selenium-based utility to download entire albums from the now defunct free-mp3-download.net website.

title: Alpha
about: Mockup of a very extendable and easy to understand programming language, eventually turned into English.

title: Chungus
about: A pure-python math engine designed to be implemented into xpp.

title: CubeRPC
about: An extremely basic Discord RPC for musikcube.

title: GArchive
about: Code for the original website for Geesecraft. Replaced by DmmD's new Svelte-based code.

title: Goose NFT
about: Jokily made terminal NFT system where a user could sign text files using a Public/Private keypair and then verify the files.

title: ISM
about: iiPython System Manager, a web UI that was similar to Grafana, allowing you to keep a quick tab on your system.

title: Modrinth Modpack Updater
about: A CLI tool that would take a JSON file containing modpack information, and automatically update all the mods to a specific Minecraft version.

title: Ooga Booga
about: URL shortener that was meant to replace JAUS, rewritten using modern async libraries and all.

title: Shuttle
about: Chat system that was (essentially) meant to be Honknet v2, a collab between me and DmmD to make a decentralized, modern chat system. It is on an indefinite hold as of May 2024.

title: UU-Extended
about: Minecraft Java mod based on Fabric to extend UU-Matter from Tech Reborn.

title: Xenon
about: A Roblox streaming engine that was meant as a no-server-required rewrite of StreamX. It could stream anything except for attachment connections, and was much easier to implement. The original devforum thread is available here.

title: Codelet
about: A code giveaway platform for Roblox, intended to supersede 'FTS' giveaways. The project was made in conjunction with pyxfluff but never published.

title: English
about: Simple to use, english language based programming language. First project to be included in the Goos.lol organization.

title: Goos.lol
about: The official Goos.lol website, which is still visible on the wayback machine.

title: iiPython Core
about: Core service for all of my data and sites, meant to be a home for datasets. I abandoned it after getting the main UI up and indexing my music.

title: Musescore Downloader
about: A site that allowed users to download sheet music from Musescore via the use of dl-librescore.

title: Textnet
about: Another attempted rewrite of PyNet, this one included a terminal rendering solution that was somewhat modernized but otherwise didn't get very far.

title: Weather Archiver
about: An old website designed to constantly record data from OpenWeatherMap, store it, and then graph it with a neat UI.

title: igame
about: A python module for making terminal games, giving access to easy audio control, menu systems, and function timeouts. Implemented in some of K4ffu's projects, otherwise not used.
Assembly-inspired programming language that required manually managing the memory stack, dropping memory, etc.

title: Acellus
about: Collection of decompiled scripts of Acellus's web interface. Allowed speeding up videos, preventing tab detection, pasting into writing tutor, and more.

title: Album Art Downloader
about: Python script that will automatically scan my music directory for any albums without cover art, and will then open MusicHoarder's cover website.

title: Anime CLI
about: TUI script that streams anime directly from torrents, originally based on Consumet. It is one of the only projects that was published by Goos.lol.

title: Assistant
about: A remake of my original Virtual Assistant, this time using ChatGPT and actually implementing features such as weather, music control, etc.

title: cdbar
about: Website that displays information about various CDs given a barcode, powered by MusicBrainz.

title: Cephei
about: Intended to be a chat system, it started as a rewrite of the currently on hold Shuttle project. I implemented a login and signup flow and then got tired of it.

title: CRS
about: Central Routing System, a websocket based FastAPI project that was intended to be used to replace REST for some of my microservices and join them together.

title: Distributed Filesystem
about: Test implementation of a distributed filesystem across several different nodes spanning different IP networks. Never completed.

title: dnsbench
about: Quick script that benchmarks local DNS servers using dnspython.

title: Emojizer
about: Discord bot that communicates with gpt4free and responds to users with emojis only.

title: Feishin
about: My personal fork of jeffvli's Feishin, an Electron-based music player. This fork includes theming changes, QOL improvements, completely revamped Discord RPC, and much more.

title: Feishin Micro
about: Miniplayer for Feishin, that floats on your desktop and allows you to control the app without opening the entire window. Includes scrolling text UI, a song visualizer, and that's about it.

title: Feishin RPC
about: External RPC application for Feishin that was meant to improve the Discord RPC experience. This was later built into my custom fork so it is no longer maintained.

title: Fiku
about: Attempted rewrite of Maloja, with the goal of being MUCH more efficient. Goal completed, although I only got like 3 pages implemented.

title: File System
about: Attempt at a blazing fast, privacy centered, and modern file sharing service.

title: Halo
about: An attempted remake of Nova with the main selling point being speed.

title: RFS
about: A remote filesystem for copying files from system to system without SSH or something that requires a lot of setup.

title: LRCup
about: Python CLI and library for interacting with the LRCLIB.net API. It also includes tools for embedding lyrics, along with general LRC tools.

title: Navidrome Play Mover
about: Script that can move track plays between albums in Navidrome.

title: ndcli
about: Navidrome browsing CLI tool, implemented in Python and then later attempted in Go. Allowed checking track/album/artist statistics from the terminal.

title: NETGEAR UI
about: Replacement interface for the NETGEAR C7800 series router, since the default one sucks.

title: Website (Modern)
about: My latest modern website, which has still gone through nearly 4 different variations at this point.

title: Nightwatch
about: A chat system similar to all the ones I've made previously, but this time with a full server, complete client, bot library, web frontend, and still actively maintained.

title: Nova
about: A site building framework built using the Python websockets module, Jinja2 and no other dependencies. It's capable of auto minification, TypeScript compilation, and more.

title: Now Playing
about: A microservice for sending now playing information from ListenBrainz in a websocket. Actively in production use on my website.

title: Feishin OBS
about: A widget to display music information from Feishin in OBS Studio. Made in a night, and never used again.

title: Pizza
about: Metadata collection system and management tool, meant to compete primarily with beets.

title: Pizza Proxy
about: Cover art proxying system for use in my fork of Feishin, which allows users to get cover art in their RPC without opening ports.

title: Statuspage
about: Simple, fast as hell, efficient on resources multi-server status page powered by Cloudflare Pages. Killed due to me no longer needing a statuspage.

title: TP Link Lightbulb
about: Basic TP-Link lightbulb controller written with Electron, since I couldn't find any better solutions.

title: USPS
about: A fully featured USPS package tracker with additional support for UPS packages. I've used it to receive nearly 15 different packages over the past year.

title: gun.
about: DESCRIPTION YET TO BE WRITTEN.

title: DmmD OS
about: An in-browser operating system based on Windows 95, designed to be ran from within DmmD's BIOS.

title: dmmd-py
about: A python library/module to wrap all of 's APIs. As of right now that just includes his iCDN.

title: Networking
about: A Cloudflare-backed site that showed HTTP(s) connection statistics and routing information.

title: PNV3
about: The final form of PyNet, an actively in development rewrite of PyNet v3, hence keeping the original acronym.

title: Radar
about: Leaflet-powered web interface for the National Weather Service's radar API, because I couldn't find a site with a clean looking dark UI.

title: Radio
about: A next generation web radio project, powered by websockets and FastAPI.

title: Sound
about: Send files with sound or die trying: encode binary into hexadecimal, send as sound waves, receive on the other end and reconstruct the file.

title: htx
about: Zero-dependency HTTP server and reverse proxy built entirely from scratch as an experiment/proof of concept. It can act as a standalone HTTP server, giving low level access to the protocol, and also has reverse proxy functionality.

title: il
about: Python module for making clean logs, boxes, lines, and everything of the sort.

title: logic
about: A logic symbol processor for PHI-1113 that I wrote in the span of a couple weeks. My professor thought it was 'pretty neat'.

title: NJROTC Autograde
about: The goal was to create an application that allows for quick and easy autograding of cadet uniforms for using in JROTC.

title: cdrip
about: Local Bun-based codebase that runs a webserver and then allows you to rip any CDs in your system using cdparanoia.

title: cdmaker
about: Tool that creates fully readable CD images (bin/cue) given a directory of WAV files. I made it because I couldn't find any other tooling that worked out of the box without needing any dependencies or weird setup beforehand, while this script only requires you to have Python 3.

title: pdb
about: This site was a pretty basic frontend for a massive ass JSON(c) file that had all my project data on it, deprecated in May 2026 when I merged it into my main site.

title: metrics
about: FastAPI based ASGI system that records Cloudflare site metrics, including TTFB, TLS Handshake Time, TLS Connection Time, and more. Can be ran from a customizable amount of nodes all around the world, with a 1-4 limit.

title: carbon
about: Asynchronous NoSQL database that uses SQLite as a backend for data storage. It has full support for replication and was a fun experiment to work on.

title: radon
about: An attempt at a decentralized network for sending encrypted data between peers.

title: ghost
about: Work-in-progress self-hosted privacy-respecting analytics server designed to provide nothing but a visit count.

title: cmd
about: Web terminal complete with games, social interaction via email, a typing test, baud rate emulation, and more.

title: rei
about: CLI meant to assist with everything from managing projects to updating DNS records.

title: genrerip
about: Tool to automatically find accurate music genres via MusicBrainz and LastFM.

title: shadow
about: HTTP/1.1 compliant ASGI system, meant to replace HTX (2508).

title: snorlax
about: A YouTube archival service, all running locally. It supports downloading videos as well as entire channels, displaying it in a fairly decent interface.

title: anilist-preview
about: DESCRIPTION YET TO BE WRITTEN.

about: DESCRIPTION YET TO BE WRITTEN.

about: DESCRIPTION YET TO BE WRITTEN.

title: iavx
about: The iiPython AV1 Encoding System. As of 03/01/26 it's been used to encode around 10 different shows.

title: sniffer
about: Automatically trace all externally linked domains on a website and display them in a node tree.

title: pixel-os
about: New work-in-progress web based operating system, with full modding support.

title: cyanide
about: Central report system for K-12 schools in Mississippi, ranking vulnerabilities by risk.
